name: Pacific Cash Advance
description_short: Pacific Cash Advance offers payday loans and short-term emergency cash in Los Angeles with same-day funding, positioned as a direct lender with no hidden fees.
description_long:
Pacific Cash Advance, L.L.C. is a licensed payday lender operating from a physical storefront in South Los Angeles at 8501 South Figueroa Street. The company positions itself as a direct lender offering short-term emergency cash solutions, primarily payday loans designed to bridge financial gaps until the borrower's next paycheck. The business operates Monday through Saturday (9 a.m. to 6 p.m. weekdays, 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. Saturday) and maintains a local phone line for customer inquiries.

Pacific Cash Advance's core offering is payday loans—short-term borrowing products marketed as solutions for immediate financial needs. According to their website, the application process is described as simple, and they emphasize being a direct lender with no hidden fees. The company also operates a referral program that compensates both customers and non-customers for successful referrals, suggesting an emphasis on word-of-mouth growth.

The company distinguishes itself through customer service claims, including fast processing times (noted as "quick service in and out" and "very fast" for repeat customers after initial verification). Their South Los Angeles location in a "bustling neighborhood" near Madeline's Fashion provides physical accessibility for the local community. The storefront model allows for same-day funding, a critical feature for emergency cash products.

As a payday lender, Pacific Cash Advance operates in a heavily regulated but high-cost lending category. While the website emphasizes transparency ("no hidden fees") and convenience, payday loans by nature carry significant APR costs that can exceed 400% annualized in California. The company does not disclose specific fee structures, APR rates, or loan terms on their website, which is standard but notable given their claim of transparency. Customer reviews are limited to two testimonials praising service speed and professionalism, offering minimal insight into pricing satisfaction or loan outcomes.
pros: Direct lender model eliminates middleman fees
Same-day or next-day funding for emergency cash needs
Physical storefront location enables walk-in service
Fast processing for repeat customers after initial verification
No hidden fees claimed explicitly on website
Referral program compensates customers for bringing in new business
Extended hours including Saturday morning availability
cons: Payday loans carry extremely high APR rates (typically 300-400%+ in California) not disclosed on website
No pricing, fee schedule, or APR information provided online; customers must call or visit in person
Limited online presence and no apparent online application or funding option
Only two customer reviews available, insufficient feedback for reputation assessment
High debt trap risk inherent to payday lending model; renewal and rollover cycles can create long-term debt
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash emergencies who cannot wait 1-2 business days for alternatives
Borrowers with poor credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit cards
South Los Angeles residents who prefer in-person transactions and local service
Repeat customers seeking fast processing after establishing relationship with the lender
diagnosis: Pacific Cash Advance is best for borrowers in genuine financial emergencies within the Los Angeles area who need same-day cash and lack access to traditional lending. The primary caveat is that payday loans, while fast, are among the most expensive consumer credit products available and can create cycles of debt if used repeatedly; the company provides no transparent pricing online, requiring customers to visit or call to understand true costs before borrowing.
